subject: Project Management
title: Edinburgh's Tram Fiasco
description: Learn Edinburgh's Tram Fiasco in one minute.

hook.text: Edinburgh planned a 22-km tram line for £375 million. They got 14 km for over £1 billion.

buildup.text: The city centre was dug up for years. Businesses closed. Residents endured constant construction chaos.

discovery.text: Disputes between the council and contractor Bilfinger Berger led to legal battles and work stoppages.

twist.text: The project was halved in scope to save money — cutting the route short of its most useful sections.

climax.text: Edinburgh's trams opened in 2014 — three years late, three times over budget, and half the planned route.

punchline.text: Half the tram. Triple the price. Classic mega-project.

quiz.question: How did Edinburgh's tram project end up?
options: Half the route at triple the budget
